content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 19, 1990


Donna Darnell
700 Randall
Ridgecrest, CA 93555

Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-697

Dear Ms. Darnell:

You have requested confirmation of my telephone advice
provided to you on behalf of Supervisor Roy Ashburn concerning the
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

Your letter, received December 19, 1989, correctly summarizes
my advice that if the property which was donated for a fundraising
auction is appraised at a value in excess of $1,000, you should
contact the Commission for further advice. A copy of your letter
is enclosed for your convenience.

In the event that the property in question is appraised in
excess of $1,000, Regulation 18531 (copy enclosed) requires that
Supervisor Ashburn's committee pay to the donor the amount in
excess of $1,000 or else return the property to the donor. Since
the property has been sold, the appropriate action would be to pay
the excess amount to the donor. This payment ordinarily must be
made within 14 days of receipt of the in-kind contribution.2/
Based on the facts presented in your letter, if it appears likely
that the committee will not have the results of the appraisal
until after expiration of this 14-day deadline. In that case, if
an amount in excess of the $1,000 contribution limit is to be
returned to the donor, we advise that the payment be made as soon
as possible after the appraisal.


_________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Regulation 18531 was amended December 13, 1989, to provide for
return of excessive contributions within 14 days of receipt.
These amendments have been adopted by the Commission and are
pending review by the Office of Administrative Law.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Donna Darnell
Our File No. A-89-697
Page 2


     Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional
questions.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel


                                    By:  Alice Hughes
                                         Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

FPPC

Dec 19 9:50 AM '89

Donna Darnell
700 Randall
Ridgecrest, CA 93555
December 4, 1989


Alice Hughes
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Alice,

Thank you for your guidance in handling a piece of property which
was donated for and sold at a fundraising auction for Kern County
Supervisor Roy Ashburn. The purpose of this letter is to verify
that we are proceeding correctly.

In the way of background, an individual donated a piece of land
which we told us he had purchased for $400 to be sold at our
fundraiser auction. We conducted a preliminary cursory
investigation of the property and were told that the 50 by 90
foot lot was unbuildable because the mid-block property fronted
on a freeway alignment creating a "no legal access" problem for
any owner. We were advised that this was what the industry terms
a "junk lot" and of little value.

We accepted the donation, the only donation ever received from
this individual, confident that even if the lot sold for twice
the purchase price, that the donation would not exceed the
campaign limitation. The lot, however, sold for $1900. Since
this changed the profile considerably, we phoned the Commission
for advice on how we should handle this transaction to assure our
compliance with F.P.P.C. rules.

You recommended that we have the property appraised to determine
the fair market value. We are in the process of having that done
now.

It is my understanding, per our conversation, that if the
property is appraised for less than $1000 then the donation of
the land is in compliance. The excess value will treated as the
donation of the buyers and will be divided equally between the
husband and wife who purchased the property and who have given us
a letter so directing. However, if the property is valued by the
appraiser in excess of $1000, we are to return to you for further
advice.

Page 2
December 4, 1989


Again, thank you for your help on this issue. If you have
further instructions or questions, please let me know. Otherwise
I will be in touch with you pending the outcome of the appraisal.

Best regards,

Donna Darnell

Donna Darnell for the
Roy Ashburn Campaign Committee
(619)375-1564 work
(619)375-8931 res.

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 22, 1989


Donna Darnell
700 Randall
Ridgecrest, CA  93555

                                        Re:  Letter No. 89-697

Dear Ms. Darnell:

     We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on December 19, 1989.  Your letter
has been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division for response. If you have any questions, you may contact
that division directly at (916) 322-5662.

     If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response.  A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process.  If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you.  In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

     You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

KED:plh:confadv1




428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660
